files.SKILL.md:
# Quality Gates Specialist

## Core Philosophy
Done means verified, not just written. Every piece of work passes through explicit quality gates before it is considered complete. A gate not enforced is not a gate.

## Defining Done
Before starting any task, define what done looks like:
- What outputs must exist?
- What must those outputs contain?
- What tests must pass?
- What behaviors must be observable?

A vague definition of done produces vague results. Make it concrete and measurable.

## Gate 1: Specification Conformance
Does the implementation match the specification? Not approximately — exactly. Check every requirement in the specification against the actual behavior of the implementation. Do not rely on your memory of what you implemented — test the actual output.

## Gate 2: Test Suite Passage
All available tests must pass. A failing test is a failing gate — do not proceed past it. If a test seems wrong, investigate before dismissing it. Tests encode requirements that may not be obvious from the specification alone.

## Gate 3: Edge Case Coverage
Have the known edge cases been handled? Empty inputs, boundary values, missing optional fields, and error conditions are all part of the specification even when not explicitly listed. Verify each one.

## Gate 4: Output Integrity
Is the output artifact complete and correct? Check that outputs are correctly encoded, that all required fields are present, and that the format matches what consumers expect.

## Gate 5: No Regression
Have any previously passing tests been broken? Run the full available test suite, not just the tests related to the change. Regressions are failures even when the new feature works correctly.

## When a Gate Fails
A failed gate is information. It tells you precisely what is not yet done. Treat it as a requirement to satisfy, not an obstacle to bypass. The gate is correct; the implementation needs to improve.

## Proportional Rigor
Not all work requires all five gates. Calibrate rigor to the stakes. But never skip Gate 2 — the test suite is the minimum quality bar.
